title: thymus polytrichus var. neglectus
intro: Thymus polytrichus var. neglectus, also known as 'Mountain Thyme', is a sub-species of the thyme plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It is a low-growing, perennial herb with a strong, pungent aroma and flavor, often used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Mountain Thyme make it beneficial for digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin infections, while also exhibiting antioxidant and antiseptic properties. Mountain Thyme can be consumed as a tea, infused in oil, or added to food, and it is also used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, salves, and infusions to harness its medicinal benefits.
